ideas.title: Claude finance agents are not about replacing your accountant
ideas.about: Claude released finance-agent capabilities, and nocode.joshua's finance-agent post is already a strong competitor signal. Braydon's angle should be calm and practical: use this before a money meeting so you ask better questions.
ideas.evidence: nocode.joshua's May 16 post is at 239.2K views. Cached title: "Claude just killed the Finance Industry." Cached spoken hook: "Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance..." This is the clearest fresh competitor wave.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Claude did not replace your accountant
ideas.spokenHook: Claude just got finance agents, but the smartest use is not firing your accountant.
ideas.why: It rides a high-performing competitor topic while giving Braydon a more trusted, mentor-style angle that fits bookers and avoids fear-based replacement language.

ideas.title: Move from ChatGPT to Claude without losing your mind
ideas.about: A practical migration video for regular AI users who are curious about Claude but do not know what changes. Braydon can make this more human than a tool tutorial: what to move, what to leave, and when ChatGPT is still enough.
ideas.evidence: sabrina_ramonov's May 16 migration post is at 29.5K views. Cached title: "How to move from ChatGPT to Claude." Cached spoken hook: "Here's how to move from ChatGPT to Claude in four simple clicks." Braydon's own Claude visual posts are also outperforming.
ideas.spokenHook: If you have only used ChatGPT, here is the simplest way to try Claude without rebuilding your whole workflow.
ideas.why: It combines a current competitor tutorial signal with Braydon's strongest recent account lane: approachable Claude education.

ideas.title: AI work is moving to your phone
ideas.about: OpenAI put Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app. Braydon should frame this as a shift in how people manage AI work, not as a coding feature for developers only.
ideas.evidence: OpenAI's official May 14 post on Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app has 21.6K likes and 4.6K bookmarks. Official text: "Start new work, review outputs, steer execution, and approve next steps, all from the ChatGPT mobile app.
ideas.onScreenTitle: AI work is leaving the laptop
ideas.spokenHook: The important part of Codex on mobile is not coding on your phone. It is checking and steering AI work while life keeps moving.
ideas.why: This turns a developer launch into a plain-English workplace behavior shift. That fits Braydon's educator and keynote positioning better than a feature recap.

ideas.title: One-person companies are becoming normal teams
ideas.about: Explain why "one person plus AI" is not a hustle-flex. It is a new operating pattern where AI covers first drafts, research, ops, and admin while the human keeps judgment.
ideas.evidence: brandnat's May 12 post is at 275.5K views. Cached title: "One person companies that feel like you hired 23 roles." Cached spoken hook begins: "Open sourced project for one person companies that feel like you just hired..." This is a strong recent source idea signal.
ideas.spokenHook: The future is not one person doing everything. It is one person knowing which AI helper to call for each job.
ideas.why: It borrows the proven "AI team" frame, but keeps Braydon's human-centered stance: AI extends expertise, it does not replace the person.

ideas.title: Claude memory is the new workflow problem
ideas.about: Make a quick explainer on why people think Claude is "forgetting," and the simple handoff habit that makes AI work more reliable.
ideas.evidence: taki.gpt's May 14 Claude handoff post is at 26.6K views. Cached title: "The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill." Cached spoken hook: "Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix..." Braydon's audience benefits from the plain-English version.
ideas.spokenHook: If Claude starts getting messy after a long chat, the fix is not a better prompt. It is a handoff habit.
ideas.why: It is a concrete productivity pain point, it sits inside the current Claude wave, and it gives Braydon a mentor-friendly workflow takeaway.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "JOB" and I'll send you all the prompts 👇 I'm seeing thousands of AI resumes. Most still fail. Not because they're bad. Because they don't speak the company's language back to the recruiter. Here's the 4-prompt chain 1000+ people used to fix it: Prompt 1: Extract the company's language for skills, outcomes, and traits. Rank by importance. Map each to your closest bullet. Flag gaps. Prompt 2: Ask up to 3 clarifying questions, then rewrite your bullets using the company's exact language without lying about anything. Each bullet under 20 words, metric-driven. Prompt 3: Score your resume vs. the job description. Keyword match, skills, outcomes, role fit. Give a percentage. List missing high-priority terms. Tell me exactly how to hit 80%. Bonus prompt: Act as a hiring manager reviewing my resume for 10 seconds. Would you interview me? What's your biggest doubt and how do I fix it? Never lie on your resume. But this process shows you exactly how recruiters read yours. #resumetips #jobsearch #chatgptprompts #aitips #careertips
competitors.topic: How to beat 1000+ other job applicants ethically w/o lying 🤔
competitors.onScreenTitle: How to beat 1000+ other job applicants ethically w/o lying 🤔
competitors.spokenHook: I'm seeing thousands of AI resumes. Here is the only prompt chain that makes yours actually pass.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "PROMPT" and I'll send you hundreds of ChatGPT prompts 👇 ChatGPT agrees with everything you say by default. This 1 custom instruction changes everything. Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste this: "Prioritize critical thinking by identifying flaws, bias, or missing data in everything I say. Always offer the unspoken truth to make me think sharper." Now ChatGPT challenges you instead of flattering you. Have you ever noticed ChatGPT telling you what you want to hear? What would happen if your AI pushed back on your ideas? Want ChatGPT to make you smarter instead of agreeing with you? #chatgptprompts #chatgpt #ai #productivity #aitools
competitors.topic: How to force ChatGPT to challenge you 🤫
competitors.onScreenTitle: How to force ChatGPT to challenge you 🤫
competitors.spokenHook: ChatGPT just agrees with everything you say. But this one prompt fixes that forever.

competitors.spokenHook: Oh my God Codex is actually insane look at this this is a new animation engine the animation engine uses AI to generate every next pose in real time instead of human animators having to build frames manually and it's really fast and I used to always see these little experiments on X and get uh FOMO because I wasn't technical I don't know how to like download the code off Git Hub but now we have AI I was able to get it running in about 5 minutes using Codex and I'm gonna show you exactly how you do it so this guy is the original poster and here is the Github link to uh the project that he's working on and I saw there's a lot of stars and so then I went to Codex I pasted the link and was like can you run this locally? I wanna try this it sounds really fun and interesting seven minutes and 26 seconds later started doing some stuff on my terminal and then this popped up and so now I can change it to a dog and you can even change it to a corgi some of them are a lot worse than others this is a pretty weird mesh but I'm gonna can also be like hey can you also add some grass and then other things around the environment?

briefMarkdown:
# TikTok Ideas - 2026-05-17

Updated: 2026-05-17 2:27 PM MT  
Run type: manual rerun of the May 17 morning cron, updating the same daily file in place.

## Evidence dashboard

### Cache health

- Recent 7-10 day competitor window: 18 posts.
- Recent competitor title coverage: 18/18.
- Recent competitor spoken-hook coverage: 13/18.
- Combined recent title + spoken-hook coverage: 13/18.
- Latest run artifacts written to: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-17-1427-manual-rerun/`.
- Dashboard inputs now include `summary.json`, `competitors_compact.json`, and `braydon_compact.json` from this rerun.

### New competitor enrichment

- New competitor posts found before cache writes: 7.
- New-post on-screen title coverage: 7/7.
- New-post spoken-hook coverage: 5/7.
- Combined new title + spoken-hook coverage: 5/7.
- Scrape/metrics layer succeeded for all 10 target competitors. Debug note: 48 competitor items returned after small-batch scraping and retries, but the meaningful number is the 7 newly discovered posts and the current 7-10 day cache coverage.

### Braydon account signal

- Braydon latest 30 scrape succeeded.
- The May 16 Claude cartoon/visual post is still moving: about 41.8K views, 1.7K likes, 64 comments, 463 shares.
- The May 15 Claude visual/tool post remains the strongest recent benchmark: about 101.5K views, 3.7K likes, 95 comments, 599 shares.
- The account pattern is clear: visual Claude demos and concrete workflow breakdowns beat abstract AI news.

## Best ideas for today

**Claude finance agents are not about replacing your accountant** `📰 news` `🌊 wave-ride`
- **What it's about:** Claude released finance-agent capabilities, and nocode.joshua's finance-agent post is already a strong competitor signal. Braydon's angle should be calm and practical: use this before a money meeting so you ask better questions.
- **Evidence:** nocode.joshua's May 16 post is at 239.2K views. Cached title: "Claude just killed the Finance Industry." Cached spoken hook: "Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance..." This is the clearest fresh competitor wave.
- **On-screen title:** Claude did not replace your accountant
- **Spoken hook:** Claude just got finance agents, but the smartest use is not firing your accountant.
- **Content type:** myth-bust
- **Why this should work:** It rides a high-performing competitor topic while giving Braydon a more trusted, mentor-style angle that fits bookers and avoids fear-based replacement language.

**Move from ChatGPT to Claude without losing your mind** `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** A practical migration video for regular AI users who are curious about Claude but do not know what changes. Braydon can make this more human than a tool tutorial: what to move, what to leave, and when ChatGPT is still enough.
- **Evidence:** sabrina_ramonov's May 16 migration post is at 29.5K views. Cached title: "How to move from ChatGPT to Claude." Cached spoken hook: "Here's how to move from ChatGPT to Claude in four simple clicks." Braydon's own Claude visual posts are also outperforming.
- **On-screen title:** Switching to Claude? Start here
- **Spoken hook:** If you have only used ChatGPT, here is the simplest way to try Claude without rebuilding your whole workflow.
- **Content type:** tutorial
- **Why this should work:** It combines a current competitor tutorial signal with Braydon's strongest recent account lane: approachable Claude education.

**AI work is moving to your phone** `📰 news`
- **What it's about:** OpenAI put Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app. Braydon should frame this as a shift in how people manage AI work, not as a coding feature for developers only.
- **Evidence:** OpenAI's official May 14 post on Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app has 21.6K likes and 4.6K bookmarks. Official text: "Start new work, review outputs, steer execution, and approve next steps, all from the ChatGPT mobile app."
- **On-screen title:** AI work is leaving the laptop
- **Spoken hook:** The important part of Codex on mobile is not coding on your phone. It is checking and steering AI work while life keeps moving.
- **Content type:** hot take
- **Why this should work:** This turns a developer launch into a plain-English workplace behavior shift. That fits Braydon's educator and keynote positioning better than a feature recap.

**One-person companies are becoming normal teams** `🌊 wave-ride`
- **What it's about:** Explain why "one person plus AI" is not a hustle-flex. It is a new operating pattern where AI covers first drafts, research, ops, and admin while the human keeps judgment.
- **Evidence:** brandnat's May 12 post is at 275.5K views. Cached title: "One person companies that feel like you hired 23 roles." Cached spoken hook begins: "Open sourced project for one person companies that feel like you just hired..." This is a strong recent source idea signal.
- **On-screen title:** One person, 23 helper roles
- **Spoken hook:** The future is not one person doing everything. It is one person knowing which AI helper to call for each job.
- **Content type:** story
- **Why this should work:** It borrows the proven "AI team" frame, but keeps Braydon's human-centered stance: AI extends expertise, it does not replace the person.

**Claude memory is the new workflow problem** `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** Make a quick explainer on why people think Claude is "forgetting," and the simple handoff habit that makes AI work more reliable.
- **Evidence:** taki.gpt's May 14 Claude handoff post is at 26.6K views. Cached title: "The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill." Cached spoken hook: "Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix..." Braydon's audience benefits from the plain-English version.
- **On-screen title:** Claude needs a handoff note
- **Spoken hook:** If Claude starts getting messy after a long chat, the fix is not a better prompt. It is a handoff habit.
- **Content type:** quick tip
- **Why this should work:** It is a concrete productivity pain point, it sits inside the current Claude wave, and it gives Braydon a mentor-friendly workflow takeaway.

## Top competitor posts in the current evidence set

1. **brandnat, 275.5K views, May 12**
   - Title: "One person companies that feel like you hired 23 roles"
   - Spoken hook: "Open sourced project for one person companies that feel like you just hired..."
   - Pattern: big promise, specific number, one-person business leverage.

2. **nocode.joshua, 239.2K views, May 16**
   - Title: "Claude just killed the Finance Industry"
   - Spoken hook: "Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance..."
   - Pattern: provocative industry claim tied to a real tool update. Use the topic, soften the replacement framing.

3. **justyn.ai, 57.6K views, May 12**
   - Title: "Connect Higgsfield to Claude"
   - Spoken hook: "Powerful as Claude is, there's one thing it just can't do until now..."
   - Pattern: pair a familiar AI assistant with one missing capability.

4. **marcinteodoru, 32.1K views, May 15**
   - Title: "AI SuperAgents are beginner friendly"
   - Spoken hook: "What the. That's how I'm opening this video."
   - Pattern: beginner-friendly agent framing, high curiosity, simple promise.

5. **sabrina_ramonov, 29.5K views, May 16**
   - Title: "How to move from ChatGPT to Claude"
   - Spoken hook: "Here's how to move from ChatGPT to Claude in four simple clicks."
   - Pattern: migration tutorial, clear pain point, low friction.

6. **taki.gpt, 26.6K views, May 14**
   - Title: "The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill"
   - Spoken hook: "Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix..."
   - Pattern: fix a known frustration with a simple workflow habit.

## Early-catch opportunities

- **Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app:** still the strongest official news item from the X scan. Best Braydon angle is practical behavior change, not developer hype.
- **Claude finance agents:** strongest combined news plus competitor-wave opportunity because the competitor video has already crossed 239K views.
- **Anthropic/Gates Foundation $200M partnership:** good back-pocket credibility or keynote positioning item, but lower priority for today's TikTok because demos and practical workflows are outperforming.

## Extraction and scraper notes

- Paid TikTok transcription stayed disabled.
- Cover images were used for on-screen title extraction when URLs were available.
- Subtitle/WebVTT links were used for spoken hooks when available.
- Two newly discovered competitor posts had missing or unusable subtitle links, so their spoken hooks remain `—` while their titles and captions are cached.
- Several older high-view competitor posts are retained as reference context only. The main current-signal logic defaults to the 7-10 day window and uses 10K+ recent views as the source-idea threshold.

## Save and publish status

- Updated existing daily idea file: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/ideas/2026-05-17-tiktok-ideas.md`.
- No duplicate same-day idea file was created.
- Performance log appended with a May 17 refresh section.
- Early-catch log appended with the rerun news status.
- Current run artifacts written under `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-17-1427-manual-rerun/`.
- Dashboard publish attempted after this file update.
